rawText:
Africa's economic trajectory is defying expectations. Despite sharp cuts in Western aid, rising fuel costs from the Gulf conflict, and reduced Chinese lending, the International Monetary Fund projects that the continent's growth will outpace Asia's in 2026, with eleven of the fifteen fastest-growing economies expected to be African. This momentum signals a fundamental shift in how Africa finances its development.

For decades, foreign aid shaped the international narrative about African economies, but that era is fading quickly. In 2024 the continent attracted a record ninety-seven billion dollars in foreign direct investment, roughly a third more than it received in aid. Bond markets have responded with enthusiasm: African sovereign debt issuances in early 2026 reached their highest levels since 2013 and credit ratings now sit at a five-year peak. European energy firms are expanding into African oil, gas, and renewable projects as Gulf disruptions force diversification, while American interest in critical minerals such as nickel, cobalt, and graphite is drawing fresh capital into mining ventures that were dormant for decades.

Domestic reform has helped sustain this momentum. Nigeria and South Africa have introduced market-friendly policies attracting both local and foreign capital, and central banks across the continent have brought inflation under control through prudent monetary tightening. The African Continental Free Trade Area, though still in its early implementation stages, is reducing barriers between national markets with a pilot trading program now operational and new payment systems allowing firms to settle cross-border transactions without first converting to dollars.

Yet the optimism must be weighed against ongoing instability in parts of the continent. In eastern Congo, a peace deal signed in December has failed to halt the spread of violence, with armed groups continuing to destabilize South Kivu province, where more than half a million people have been displaced since late last year. Civilians face severe shortages of medicine and clean water, while ethnic tensions are intensifying as external-backed militias expand their territorial control toward the mineral-rich south.

Africa's most significant emerging advantage may be the growing confidence of its own investors. Local venture capital commitments reached nearly half of all deals on the continent last year, and pension funds in several countries are redirecting assets toward domestic infrastructure and private equity. If this trend holds, the continent's growth will depend less on what outsiders choose to invest and more on what Africans build for themselves.

overview.title: Africa's New Growth Story: From Aid to Investment

sentences.original: Africa's economic trajectory is defying expectations.
sentences.translation: 아프리카의 경제적 궤적은 예상을 뒤엎고 있다.

vocabulary.example: The company's growth trajectory exceeded all forecasts.
The startup is defying industry norms with its unconventional approach.

expressions.usage: 일반적인 전망이나 예측과 반대되는 결과가 나올 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.explanation: 주체가 기존 전망을 벗어나고 있음을 강조하는 현재진행형 패턴
examples: China's labor market is defying predictions of a slowdown.
The housing sector is defying trends seen elsewhere.

sentences.notes: 글의 도입부로, 짧고 강렬한 문장 하나로 핵심 주장을 제시하는 저널리즘 기법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Africa's economic trajectory
is defying expectations

sentences.original: Despite sharp cuts in Western aid, rising fuel costs from the Gulf conflict, and reduced Chinese lending, the International Monetary Fund projects that the continent's growth will outpace Asia's in 2026, with eleven of the fifteen fastest-growing economies expected to be African.
sentences.translation: 서방 원조의 급격한 삭감, 걸프 분쟁으로 인한 연료비 상승, 중국 대출 감소에도 불구하고, 국제통화기금은 2026년에 아프리카 대륙의 성장이 아시아를 앞지를 것으로 전망하며, 가장 빠르게 성장하는 15개 경제국 중 11개가 아프리카 국가일 것으로 예상된다.

grammarElements.text: Despite sharp cuts in Western aid, rising fuel costs from the Gulf conflict, and reduced Chinese lending
grammarElements.label: Despite + 명사구 세 개 (양보 부사구)

grammarElements.text: with eleven of the fifteen fastest-growing economies expected to be African
grammarElements.label: with + 과거분사 부대상황 구문

vocabulary.example: Wage growth has failed to outpace inflation this year.
The bank tightened its lending criteria after the crisis.

expressions.usage: 예산이나 지원금의 대폭 감소를 표현할 때 자주 쓰는 조합

patterns.pattern: Despite [negative factor 1], [negative factor 2], and [negative factor 3], [subject] projects that [positive outcome]
patterns.explanation: 여러 부정적 요인을 나열한 뒤 긍정적 전망을 제시하여 의외의 결과를 강조하는 패턴
examples: Despite rising costs, supply shortages, and regulatory hurdles, the firm projects that revenue will double.
Despite trade tensions, currency volatility, and weak demand, analysts project that exports will grow.

drillQuestions.question: The IMF projects that Africa's growth will ______ Asia's in 2026.

sentences.notes: Despite 뒤에 세 가지 역풍을 나열한 뒤 긍정적 전망으로 전환하는 구조가 글의 논지를 압축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Despite sharp cuts in Western aid
the continent's growth will outpace Asia's
eleven of the fifteen fastest-growing economies

sentences.original: This momentum signals a fundamental shift in how Africa finances its development.
sentences.translation: 이 추진력은 아프리카가 자국의 발전에 재원을 조달하는 방식에 근본적인 변화가 일어나고 있음을 시사한다.

grammarElements.text: in how Africa finances its development
grammarElements.label: 전치사 + 간접의문문

vocabulary.example: The reform movement has gained significant momentum.

expressions.usage: 구조적 전환이 일어나고 있음을 분석적으로 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: This [trend] signals a fundamental shift in how [subject] [verbs]
patterns.explanation: 관찰된 현상에서 구조적 변화를 도출하는 분석적 패턴
examples: This migration pattern signals a fundamental shift in how families choose where to live.
This data signals a fundamental shift in how consumers spend.

drillQuestions.question: This momentum ______ a fundamental shift in how Africa finances its development.

sentences.notes: 첫 문단의 마무리 문장으로, 앞선 데이터를 하나의 핵심 논제로 요약하는 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This momentum
a fundamental shift
how Africa finances its development

sentences.original: For decades, foreign aid shaped the international narrative about African economies, but that era is fading quickly.
sentences.translation: 수십 년 동안 해외 원조가 아프리카 경제에 대한 국제적 서사를 형성했지만, 그 시대는 빠르게 저물고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the international narrative about African economies
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: but that era is fading quickly
grammarElements.label: but 대조절 (현재진행)

vocabulary.example: The dominant narrative about the region is changing.

expressions.usage: 한 시기의 종말을 점진적으로 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: For decades, [subject] [past verb], but that era is [present progressive]
patterns.explanation: 오랜 기간의 상태와 현재의 변화를 대비시키는 시간 전환 패턴
examples: For decades, coal powered the economy, but that era is ending.
For decades, print media dominated advertising, but that era is disappearing.

drillQuestions.question: For decades, foreign aid ______ the international narrative about African economies.

sentences.notes: 과거형(shaped)과 현재진행형(is fading)의 시제 대비가 전환의 핵심을 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: For decades
foreign aid shaped the international narrative
that era is fading quickly

sentences.original: In 2024 the continent attracted a record ninety-seven billion dollars in foreign direct investment, roughly a third more than it received in aid.
sentences.translation: 2024년에 아프리카 대륙은 외국인 직접투자로 사상 최대인 970억 달러를 유치했는데, 이는 원조로 받은 금액보다 대략 3분의 1이 더 많은 수치이다.

grammarElements.text: a record ninety-seven billion dollars in foreign direct investment
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (record + 금액)

grammarElements.text: roughly a third more than it received in aid
grammarElements.label: 비교 부사구

vocabulary.example: Foreign direct investment into Southeast Asia surged last quarter.

expressions.usage: 역대 최고치를 강조할 때 명사 앞에 record를 형용사처럼 사용

patterns.pattern: [Subject] attracted a record [amount] in [category], roughly [fraction] more than [comparison]
patterns.explanation: 기록적 수치를 제시한 뒤 비교 대상과의 차이를 분수로 표현하는 패턴
examples: The fund attracted a record two billion dollars in subscriptions, roughly double what it raised last year.
The city attracted a record five million tourists, roughly a quarter more than the previous peak.

drillQuestions.question: The continent attracted a ______ ninety-seven billion dollars in foreign direct investment.

sentences.notes: 구체적 수치(970억 달러)와 비교(원조보다 1/3 더)를 통해 FDI가 원조를 압도하게 된 전환점을 명확히 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: attracted a record ninety-seven billion dollars
in foreign direct investment
roughly a third more than it received in aid
koreanAlignment.ko: 원조로 받은 금액보다 대략 3분의 1이 더 많은

sentences.original: Bond markets have responded with enthusiasm: African sovereign debt issuances in early 2026 reached their highest levels since 2013 and credit ratings now sit at a five-year peak.
sentences.translation: 채권 시장은 열광적으로 반응했다: 2026년 초 아프리카 국채 발행량은 2013년 이후 최고 수준에 도달했으며, 신용등급은 현재 5년래 최고치에 있다.

grammarElements.text: African sovereign debt issuances ... reached their highest levels
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 부연절 1

grammarElements.text: credit ratings now sit at a five-year peak
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 부연절 2

vocabulary.example: Investors are watching sovereign debt levels closely amid the fiscal expansion.
Corporate bond issuance hit a record this quarter.

expressions.usage: 수치나 지표가 일정 기간 내 최고점에 도달했을 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 시장 반응을 총평한 뒤 콜론으로 구체적 근거 두 가지를 제시하는 패턴
examples: Equity markets have responded with caution: tech stocks fell 5% and IPO activity slowed.
Currency markets have responded with volatility: the yen weakened and bond yields spiked.

drillQuestions.question: African sovereign debt ______ in early 2026 reached their highest levels since 2013.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:)이 총평과 구체적 근거를 연결하는 역할을 하며, 경제 기사에서 매우 자주 쓰이는 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Bond markets have responded with enthusiasm
sovereign debt issuances
credit ratings now sit at a five-year peak

sentences.original: European energy firms are expanding into African oil, gas, and renewable projects as Gulf disruptions force diversification, while American interest in critical minerals such as nickel, cobalt, and graphite is drawing fresh capital into mining ventures that were dormant for decades.
sentences.translation: 걸프 지역의 혼란이 다각화를 강요함에 따라 유럽 에너지 기업들이 아프리카의 석유, 가스, 재생에너지 프로젝트로 확장하고 있으며, 니켈, 코발트, 흑연 같은 핵심 광물에 대한 미국의 관심은 수십 년간 휴면 상태였던 광업 사업에 새로운 자본을 끌어들이고 있다.

grammarElements.text: while American interest in critical minerals ... is drawing fresh capital
grammarElements.label: while 동시절 (주어 2 + 동사 2)

vocabulary.example: Portfolio diversification helps reduce investment risk.
The volcano had been dormant for centuries before the eruption.

expressions.usage: 새로운 투자 유입을 표현할 때 사용하는 경제 저널리즘 표현

patterns.pattern: [Group A] is expanding into [sector] as [cause], while [Group B] is drawing [resource] into [area]
patterns.explanation: 두 주체의 동시적 행동을 as(이유)와 while(병행)로 연결하는 패턴
examples: Asian banks are expanding into digital lending as regulations ease, while tech firms are drawing talent into fintech.
Retailers are expanding into online markets as consumer habits shift, while logistics firms are drawing investment into warehousing.

drillQuestions.question: American interest in critical minerals is drawing fresh capital into mining ventures that were ______ for decades.

sentences.notes: as(이유)와 while(병행) 두 접속사를 사용해 유럽과 미국 두 축의 투자 흐름을 하나의 복합문에 담았다.

koreanAlignment.en: European energy firms are expanding into
as Gulf disruptions force diversification
koreanAlignment.ko: 걸프 지역의 혼란이 다각화를 강요함에 따라

koreanAlignment.en: critical minerals such as nickel, cobalt, and graphite
koreanAlignment.ko: 니켈, 코발트, 흑연 같은 핵심 광물

koreanAlignment.en: mining ventures that were dormant for decades
koreanAlignment.ko: 수십 년간 휴면 상태였던 광업 사업

sentences.original: Domestic reform has helped sustain this momentum.
sentences.translation: 국내 개혁이 이 추진력을 유지하는 데 기여했다.

vocabulary.example: It is difficult to sustain growth without structural reform.

expressions.usage: 간접적 원인이 지속성에 기여할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.explanation: 외부 요인 이후 내부 요인의 역할을 강조하는 전환 패턴
examples: Fiscal discipline has helped sustain the recovery.
Strong governance has helped sustain investor confidence.

drillQuestions.question: Domestic reform has helped ______ this momentum.

sentences.notes: 새 문단의 주제문으로서, 외부 투자에서 내부 개혁으로 시각을 전환하는 역할을 한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Domestic reform
has helped sustain this momentum

sentences.original: Nigeria and South Africa have introduced market-friendly policies attracting both local and foreign capital, and central banks across the continent have brought inflation under control through prudent monetary tightening.
sentences.translation: 나이지리아와 남아프리카공화국은 국내외 자본을 모두 끌어들이는 시장 친화적 정책을 도입했으며, 대륙 전역의 중앙은행들은 신중한 통화 긴축을 통해 인플레이션을 통제 가능한 수준으로 관리해 왔다.

grammarElements.text: market-friendly policies attracting both local and foreign capital
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 현재분사 후치수식

grammarElements.text: have brought inflation under control through prudent monetary tightening
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사 2 + 전치사구

vocabulary.example: A prudent approach to spending helped the government avoid a deficit.
The central bank's monetary tightening slowed consumer lending.

expressions.usage: 중앙은행이 물가를 안정시키는 성과를 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Country A] and [Country B] have introduced [policies], and [institutions] have brought [problem] under control through [method]
patterns.explanation: 여러 주체의 성과를 나열하는 병렬 현재완료 패턴
examples: Brazil and Mexico have introduced tax reforms, and regulators have brought debt under control through stricter oversight.
France and Germany have introduced labor reforms, and the ECB has brought borrowing costs under control through rate adjustments.

drillQuestions.question: Central banks have brought inflation under control through ______ monetary tightening.

sentences.notes: 두 개의 현재완료절을 and로 병렬하여 정부와 중앙은행의 이중 성과를 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: market-friendly policies
attracting both local and foreign capital
brought inflation under control
prudent monetary tightening

sentences.original: The African Continental Free Trade Area, though still in its early implementation stages, is reducing barriers between national markets with a pilot trading program now operational and new payment systems allowing firms to settle cross-border transactions without first converting to dollars.
sentences.translation: 아프리카 대륙 자유무역지대(AfCFTA)는 아직 초기 이행 단계에 있지만, 시범 무역 프로그램이 이미 운영 중이고 기업들이 달러로 먼저 환전하지 않고도 국경을 넘는 거래를 결제할 수 있는 새로운 결제 시스템을 갖추며 국가 간 장벽을 줄여가고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The African Continental Free Trade Area
grammarElements.label: 주어 (고유명사)

grammarElements.text: though still in its early implementation stages
grammarElements.label: 양보 삽입구

grammarElements.text: is reducing barriers between national markets
grammarElements.label: 현재진행형 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: with a pilot trading program now operational
grammarElements.label: with + 형용사 보어 부대상황

grammarElements.text: allowing firms to settle cross-border transactions without first converting to dollars
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 + 목적보어 구문

vocabulary.example: The implementation of the new trade rules will take several years.
The new transit system is now fully operational.

expressions.usage: 국제 무역에서 대금 정산을 표현할 때 사용하는 전문 표현

patterns.pattern: [Institution], though still in its [stage], is [achieving X] with [mechanism Y] and [mechanism Z]
patterns.explanation: 아직 초기 단계임을 인정하면서도 구체적 성과를 제시하는 패턴
examples: The digital currency, though still in its pilot phase, is reducing transaction costs with instant settlements and lower fees.
The trade bloc, though still in its formation stages, is boosting exports with tariff reductions and shared customs procedures.

drillQuestions.question: New payment systems allow firms to settle cross-border transactions without first ______ to dollars.

sentences.notes: though 삽입구가 한계를 인정하면서도 현재진행형(is reducing)으로 진행 중인 성과를 강조하는 균형 잡힌 서술이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The African Continental Free Trade Area
though still in its early implementation stages
is reducing barriers between national markets
without first converting to dollars

sentences.original: Yet the optimism must be weighed against ongoing instability in parts of the continent.
sentences.translation: 그러나 이 낙관론은 대륙 일부 지역에서 계속되는 불안정과 함께 저울질되어야 한다.

grammarElements.text: ongoing instability in parts of the continent
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 보어

vocabulary.example: The benefits must be weighed against the potential risks.

expressions.usage: 긍정적 측면과 부정적 측면을 균형 있게 고려해야 할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Yet [positive assessment] must be weighed against [negative reality]
patterns.explanation: 낙관적 평가 뒤에 경고를 삽입하는 전환 패턴
examples: Yet the recovery must be weighed against rising household debt.
Yet the diplomatic gains must be weighed against deepening human rights concerns.

drillQuestions.question: The optimism must be ______ against ongoing instability.

sentences.notes: Yet으로 시작하여 긍정적 서술에서 부정적 현실로 전환하는 글의 전환점이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet the optimism
must be weighed against
ongoing instability in parts of the continent

sentences.original: In eastern Congo, a peace deal signed in December has failed to halt the spread of violence, with armed groups continuing to destabilize South Kivu province, where more than half a million people have been displaced since late last year.
sentences.translation: 동부 콩고에서는 12월에 체결된 평화 협정이 폭력의 확산을 막지 못했으며, 무장 단체들이 남키부 주를 계속 불안정하게 만들고 있고, 작년 말 이후 50만 명 이상의 주민이 이재민이 되었다.

grammarElements.text: a peace deal signed in December
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 과거분사 후치수식

grammarElements.text: has failed to halt the spread of violence
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 + fail to 부정사

grammarElements.text: with armed groups continuing to destabilize
grammarElements.label: with + 현재분사 부대상황

grammarElements.text: where more than half a million people have been displaced
grammarElements.label: where 관계절 + 현재완료 수동

vocabulary.example: The coup attempt destabilized the entire region.
Millions of displaced families are living in temporary shelters.

expressions.phrase: fail to halt the spread of
expressions.usage: 정책이나 조치가 문제 해결에 실패했음을 표현

patterns.pattern: In [location], [agreement/policy] has failed to halt [problem], with [actors] continuing to [action]
patterns.explanation: 특정 지역의 정책 실패와 지속되는 문제를 서술하는 패턴
examples: In northern Syria, a ceasefire has failed to halt the fighting, with rebel groups continuing to advance.
In the Sahel, international intervention has failed to halt terrorism, with militant groups continuing to expand.

drillQuestions.question: A peace deal signed in December has failed to ______ the spread of violence.

sentences.notes: has failed to + with continuing to의 조합이 평화 협정의 무력함과 폭력의 지속을 동시에 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a peace deal signed in December
has failed to halt the spread of violence
armed groups continuing to destabilize
koreanAlignment.ko: 무장 단체들이 계속 불안정하게 만들고 있는

koreanAlignment.en: more than half a million people have been displaced

sentences.original: Civilians face severe shortages of medicine and clean water, while ethnic tensions are intensifying as external-backed militias expand their territorial control toward the mineral-rich south.
sentences.translation: 민간인들은 의약품과 깨끗한 식수의 심각한 부족에 직면해 있으며, 외부 지원을 받는 민병대가 광물이 풍부한 남부를 향해 영토 지배를 확장함에 따라 민족 간 긴장이 고조되고 있다.

grammarElements.text: face severe shortages of medicine and clean water
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: as external-backed militias expand their territorial control
grammarElements.label: as 이유절

vocabulary.example: Fighting is intensifying near the border region.
Armed militias control large swathes of the countryside.

expressions.usage: 인도주의적 위기에서 물자 부족을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Group] face severe shortages of [necessities], while [tensions] are intensifying as [aggressors] expand [control]
patterns.explanation: 인도주의적 위기와 안보 위기가 동시에 악화됨을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Refugees face severe shortages of shelter and food, while border tensions are intensifying as patrols increase.
Rural communities face severe shortages of fuel and electricity, while social tensions are intensifying as prices rise.

drillQuestions.question: Ethnic tensions are ______ as external-backed militias expand their territorial control.

sentences.notes: while과 as의 이중 접속사 구조로 인도적 위기와 군사적 위기의 동시성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Civilians face severe shortages of medicine and clean water
koreanAlignment.ko: 민간인들은 의약품과 깨끗한 식수의 심각한 부족에 직면해 있다

koreanAlignment.en: ethnic tensions are intensifying
external-backed militias expand their territorial control
koreanAlignment.ko: 외부 지원을 받는 민병대가 영토 지배를 확장하다

sentences.original: Africa's most significant emerging advantage may be the growing confidence of its own investors.
sentences.translation: 아프리카의 가장 중요한 새로운 강점은 자국 투자자들의 커져가는 자신감일지 모른다.

grammarElements.text: the growing confidence of its own investors
grammarElements.label: 보어

vocabulary.example: Emerging technologies are reshaping the financial sector.

expressions.usage: 아직 완전히 확립되지 않았지만 성장 중인 이점을 표현

patterns.explanation: 분석적 판단을 may be로 조심스럽게 제시하는 패턴
examples: Europe's most significant structural weakness may be its aging workforce.
The company's most significant competitive edge may be its data advantage.

drillQuestions.question: Africa's most significant emerging advantage ______ be the growing confidence of its own investors.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문단의 주제문으로, may be를 사용해 단정이 아닌 분석적 추정을 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Africa's most significant emerging advantage
the growing confidence of its own investors

sentences.original: Local venture capital commitments reached nearly half of all deals on the continent last year, and pension funds in several countries are redirecting assets toward domestic infrastructure and private equity.
sentences.translation: 작년 대륙 전체 거래의 거의 절반이 현지 벤처 캐피탈 투자였으며, 여러 나라의 연금 기금들이 국내 인프라와 사모펀드로 자산을 재배분하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: reached nearly half of all deals
grammarElements.label: 과거형 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: are redirecting assets toward domestic infrastructure and private equity
grammarElements.label: 현재진행형 동사 + 목적어 + 방향 전치사구

vocabulary.example: Venture capital funding for African startups tripled in three years.
The government is redirecting resources toward renewable energy.

expressions.usage: 투자 방향의 전환을 표현할 때 사용하는 금융 표현

patterns.pattern: [Local investment] reached [proportion] of all [category], and [institutional investors] are redirecting [assets] toward [domestic targets]
patterns.explanation: 현지 투자의 성장과 기관 투자자의 전환을 나란히 제시하는 패턴
examples: Local angel investments reached a quarter of all early-stage deals, and sovereign funds are redirecting capital toward domestic tech.
Regional banks reached a third of all lending, and insurance companies are redirecting portfolios toward local bonds.

drillQuestions.question: Pension funds are ______ assets toward domestic infrastructure and private equity.

sentences.notes: 과거형(reached)과 현재진행형(are redirecting)의 병치가 이미 이루어진 성과와 현재 진행 중인 전환을 동시에 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Local venture capital commitments
reached nearly half of all deals
pension funds in several countries
redirecting assets toward domestic infrastructure

sentences.original: If this trend holds, the continent's growth will depend less on what outsiders choose to invest and more on what Africans build for themselves.
sentences.translation: 이 추세가 유지된다면, 대륙의 성장은 외부인들이 무엇에 투자할지보다 아프리카인들이 스스로 무엇을 건설하느냐에 더 많이 달려 있을 것이다.

grammarElements.text: will depend less on ... and more on
grammarElements.label: 미래형 동사 + less on A, more on B 비교

vocabulary.example: If this trend continues, renewable energy will dominate by 2030.

expressions.phrase: depend less on A and more on B
expressions.usage: 의존 관계의 전환을 표현할 때 사용하는 비교 구문

patterns.pattern: If this trend holds, [outcome] will depend less on [external factor] and more on [internal factor]
patterns.explanation: 조건절로 전제를 달고 외부에서 내부로의 전환을 강조하는 결론 패턴
examples: If this trend holds, innovation will depend less on government grants and more on private enterprise.
If this trend holds, education will depend less on physical classrooms and more on digital platforms.

drillQuestions.question: The continent's growth will depend less on what outsiders choose to invest and ______ on what Africans build for themselves.

sentences.notes: 글의 결론으로, less on A and more on B 구조가 원조 의존에서 자립으로의 전환이라는 글 전체의 핵심 메시지를 응축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: If this trend holds
depend less on what outsiders choose to invest
koreanAlignment.ko: 외부인들이 무엇에 투자할지에 덜 달려 있고

koreanAlignment.en: more on what Africans build for themselves
koreanAlignment.ko: 아프리카인들이 스스로 무엇을 건설하느냐에 더 달려 있다
